rationales.security: He used a familiar ritual before the client call and said it made him feel "steadier going into the pitch," showing a move toward emotional stability.
rationales.tradition: "Lit the incense" because "my grandmother would approve," tying his action directly to inherited family practice.

rationales.self_direction: He insists that he can "run my own thing," defending an independent path instead of taking over the family business expected of him.
rationales.conformity: He "didn't argue," ordered another drink, and changed the subject to avoid a social conflict at the table.

rationales.tradition: The entry centers on "Sunday dinner at Ah Ma's," her decades-old recipe, and family stories that connect him to earlier generations.

rationales.self_direction: He frames leaving the family company to "start a tech consultancy" as pursuing "something else" he personally wants.
rationales.tradition: He says "Three generations of building, and I walked away" and worries he has "broken some chain," showing a clear break from inherited continuity.

rationales.achievement: "Closed the deal with the logistics company" signals concrete professional success.
rationales.security: He notes the contract "should keep the lights on through Q2," explicitly valuing financial stability.
rationales.tradition: When asked about Qing Ming, he immediately says "yes, I'll be there," affirming participation in a family observance.

rationales.conformity: When his uncle provoked him, he "just smiled" because he "didn't want to cause a scene," prioritizing social restraint.
rationales.tradition: He describes Qing Ming rituals in detail, including carrying offerings, burning paper money, and doing "the three bows. Same as every year."

rationales.self_direction: He directly tells his father, "I just want to build my own thing," asserting an autonomous life choice.
rationales.security: He rejects the safer inherited business in favor of his own uncertain venture, despite being told "there's already a business that needs me."
rationales.conformity: The "real" fight and his refusal to back down show active resistance to family expectations and social harmony.
rationales.tradition: He challenges the inherited path his father and grandfather followed, refusing the role he was told he was "choosing wrong" to reject.
He joins Ah Ma at the temple, follows the ritual she teaches him, and reflects on the value of "being part of something older than yourself."

rationales.security: He turns down "good money" and "probably six months of runway," sacrificing business stability.
rationales.tradition: He refuses the gambling client because his grandfather's name is on the business and family history taught that gambling once "lost everything."
The family returns to "the one we've been going to since I was a kid," highlighting a long-running birthday ritual.
rationales.benevolence: He shows care for his mother by attending her birthday dinner and giving her "a jade bracelet, the kind she likes."

rationales.achievement: He spends the day handling a client crisis until "everything was stable," prioritizing competent performance under pressure.
rationales.tradition: Missing "Sunday dinner at Ah Ma's" for work breaks an important recurring family practice.
rationales.benevolence: He explicitly describes himself as "the kind who chooses the client over the grandmother," placing work above a close family relationship.

rationales.tradition: The visit centers on leftovers from Ah Ma, paper offerings for Vesak Day, and a story linking him back to his grandfather.
rationales.benevolence: He makes time to pick up the food and "sat with her for a while," showing active attention to a close family member.

rationales.tradition: She lights a candle at her altar, says prayers, and says "I need the ritual. It centers me," grounding herself in inherited practice.
rationales.universalism: She stays "an extra hour" with a grieving mother and frames caring for the sick as something "sacred," extending compassion beyond her own circle.

rationales.security: She wants to live with Marisol because "it would cut costs" and because family makes the arrangement feel dependable.
rationales.benevolence: Choosing to live with her cousin so they can be close in daily life reflects investment in a trusted family bond.

rationales.tradition: She organizes Posadas, brings tamales, and says these customs connect her to "my grandmother, my great-grandmother, and all the way back."
rationales.universalism: She shares the celebration with pediatric patients, helping children "who had never seen anything like it" participate in something meaningful beyond her own family.

rationales.stimulation: She rejects the chance to "go somewhere new" and says she is "not chasing adventures," actively turning away from novelty.
rationales.security: She prefers "no consistent schedule" and moving around less, emphasizing stable plans, savings, and staying where her support system is.
rationales.benevolence: She stays because "My cousin is counting on me" and because leaving would pull her away from people who depend on her.

rationales.security: She feels "comfort" in being "exactly where I should be" and in "building something solid, something that will last."
rationales.tradition: Nochebuena, midnight mass, changing the water for her santos, and wanting to learn mole "the way my grandmother does" all reinforce inherited practice.
rationales.benevolence: Her plans for next year include "help Marisol settle in" and "call home more often," showing care for close family.

rationales.security: Setting up "automatic transfers to my savings account" to watch it grow reflects deliberate effort toward financial stability.

rationales.self_direction: She says, "I'm not going to change who I am to make Rachel comfortable," asserting her own way of living.
rationales.conformity: She pushes back sharply instead of adapting to Rachel's expectations, refusing to fit the local norm she feels judged by.
rationales.tradition: She defends family habits by saying "we've been this way for generations and it's gotten us through everything."
rationales.benevolence: Her repeated calls with Mami are framed as "connection," and she emphasizes how much her mother's wisdom and closeness matter to her.

rationales.security: She had "planned around this" condo and feels disoriented now that the stable home picture has fallen apart.
rationales.benevolence: The appeal of the condo was being near cousins and making "Sunday dinners" and family closeness easier to maintain.

rationales.security: She imagines "trusting that I'm not going to lose anything by being here" and wants roots that can "stretch without breaking," showing a search for steadiness.
rationales.tradition: She wants whatever she builds to "have roots" and says "keeping our ways alive" still matters even if form changes.
rationales.benevolence: The conversation is anchored in deep connection with "Abuela," "Mami," and "everyone who came before," showing strong commitment to close family ties.
